display.purpose: Hero promises and named project moments.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single decisive action on a screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display:inline-flex; gap:2.6rem; align-items:center; background:#ff4e00; color:#fff6ea; border:0; border-radius:0; padding:1.05rem 1.35rem; font-family:'Avenir Next','Helvetica Neue','Gill Sans',sans-serif; font-size:.78rem; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:.12em; text-transform:uppercase; transition:background-color 180ms ease,transform 180ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:#e94500; transform:translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #fff6ea; outline-offset:3px; }

components.name: Secondary Navigation Button
components.description: A low-emphasis sign-in or utility action.
components.css: .ds-nav-button { display:inline-block; border:1px solid #c97940; border-radius:0; padding:.75rem 1rem; color:#fff6ea; font-family:'Avenir Next','Helvetica Neue','Gill Sans',sans-serif; font-size:.76rem; letter-spacing:.08em; text-decoration:none; text-transform:uppercase; transition:background-color 180ms ease,color 180ms ease; } .ds-nav-button:hover { background:#c97940; color:#090908; } .ds-nav-button:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #fff6ea; outline-offset:3px; }

components.name: Pinned Concept Board
components.description: A featured floor-plan or site-plan artifact with physical presence.
components.css: .ds-plan-board { display:grid; gap:2rem; min-height:220px; padding:1.25rem; background:#171512; color:#fff6ea; border:1px solid rgba(201,121,64,.58); border-radius:0; box-shadow:10px 11px 0 rgba(20,18,16,.82); font-family:'Avenir Next','Gill Sans',sans-serif; } .ds-plan-board span { color:#c97940; font-size:.65rem; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:.12em; } .ds-plan-board strong { align-self:end; font-family:'Iowan Old Style','Palatino Linotype',Palatino,serif; font-size:2rem; font-weight:400; }

narrative.overview: BrickPilot is a dark-only residential feasibility studio. It should feel like an architect’s calm final review: black drafting material, warm ivory type, copper geometry, and one decisive orange action.
keyCharacteristics: Quiet black surfaces with structural copper rules.
A classical display serif against a precise humanist sans.
Plan boards and measurements used as real product artifacts, never decoration.
Orange reserved for the primary action and the most important decision.

rules.name: The One Orange Rule
rules.body: Orange must occupy less than 10% of a screen. It is a decision signal, never a background theme.

rules.name: The Drafting Hierarchy Rule
rules.body: Display serif carries the human promise; labels and measurements carry the proof.

rules.name: The Pinned Artifact Rule
rules.body: Never put a wide soft shadow behind a bordered card. A surface is either flat or physically pinned.

dos: Do use #ff4e00 only for a primary decision, active constraint, or a single headline terminal mark.
Do keep every surface square, measured, and materially distinct.
Do preserve high contrast: Warm Ivory on Graphite Ink.
donts: Don't reintroduce light, cream, sand, or paper page backgrounds. BrickPilot is dark-only.
Don't use purple gradients, neon accents, glassmorphism, or pill-shaped SaaS controls.
Don't use rounded cards, soft ghost shadows, decorative grid overlays, or colored side stripes.
